mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending expectations, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint strength of housing starts and real GDP growth indicates robust aggregate demand expansion, which supports corporate earnings revisions and lifts S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST × GDPC1) shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation coupled with elevated policy rates compresses equity valuations by increasing the real discount rate and signaling future earnings headwinds, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously over a 20-day window.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy business investment, which correlates positively with S&P 500 performance over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO changes remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in residential investment exceeding 2% signals a sudden cooling in the housing sector, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly negative following months where RSAFS drops by more than 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll growth combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, driving positive earnings expectations and S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a directional accuracy above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1200 thousand units, it acts as a leading indicator for construction-related economic activity, boosting S&P 500 returns over the next week specifically during housing-led recoveries.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns show no positive skew when HOUST crosses above 1200 during identified housing-led recovery periods.
